ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Complaint Ccp Section 581f2
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

*No appearance is made in this matter at the time it was first called. Self-represented plaintiff Cynthia Servetnick appears late and on her own behalf.

Court states findings as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

On calendar:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Complaint Ccp Section 581f2

Court states findings as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Complaint CCP Section 581(f)(2) is GRANTED. Grant. No opposition filed.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.

***After the hearing the matter is recalled for late appearing plaintiff. Ruling stands. Court informs plaintiff of ruling.***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Continued To Jun-17-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Cynthia Servetnick, T:415.794.0566, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: The tentative ruling was not contested. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, modified per plaintiff's request, to allow plaintiff 60 days leave to amend, as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with 60 days leave to amend.

The City argues primarily that to the extent plaintiff seeks to state claims under FEHA, her claims are time-barred because plaintiff failed to file within one year from the date of her right-to-sue notice from the DFEH, as required by Gov. Code 12965(b). The City is wrong. Plaintiff's right to sue letter was issued July 13, 2018. (Compl. 4, Ex. A.) The one-year period ended on July 13, 2019, which was a Saturday. Plaintiff timely filed this complaint on the next business day, Monday, July 15, 2019. The complaint was timely under Code of Civ. Proc. 12a: "If the last day for the performance of any act provided or required by law to be performed within a specified period of time is a holiday, then that period is hereby extended to and including the next day that is not a holiday . . . This section applies . . . to all other provisions of law providing or requiring an act to be performed on a particular day or within a specified period of time, whether expressed in this or any other code or statute, ordinance, rule, or regulation." "Section 12a states a general rule regarding the computation of the time allowed for doing an act provided or required by law. [Citations.] . . . Section 12a applies to statutes of limitation in general . . . ." (DeLeon v. Bay Area Rapid Transit Dist. (1983) 33 Cal.3d 456, 461 [complaint against transit district for personal injuries was not barred by six-month statute of limitations in Gov. Code 945.6; although the complaint was filed one day beyond the six-month time limit, it was nonetheless timely because under 12a, the time period was extended by one day since the last day fell on a holiday (Christmas)]; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 1303, 1313 [where one-year period within which minors had to present application to file late claim under former Tort Claims Act expired on a Saturday, "the application presented on the following Monday . . . was timely."].)

However, the complaint fails to comply with elementary pleading requirements, because it fails to allege the elements of any of the proposed eight causes of action, and does not even indicate whether those claims are statutory or tort claims. Plaintiff, who is currently representing herself in propria persona, may not wait until she is able to obtain counsel in order to file a proper amended complaint. (See Yvanova v. New Century Mortgage Corp. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 495, 500 ["A party may choose to act as his or her own attorney, but 'such a party is to be treated like any other party and is entitled to the same, but no greater consideration than other litigants and attorneys.' [Citation.] As with attorneys, in propria persona litigants must follow correct rules of procedure."].) Plaintiff has 60 days leave to amend.

The Court modified and signed defendant's proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Continued To Jan-29-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-04-2019 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lisa Berkowitz, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3825, for defendant/moving party;
Cynthia Servetnick, T:415.794.0566, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: Plaintiff requests that the matter be continued to allow her to obtain legal representation. Ms. Berkowitz declares that a continuance does not prejudice the defendant. The parties stipulated to a briefing schedule. For good cause, the Court orders that:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to February 26, 2020. Plaintiff shall file its opposition by February 11, 2020. The Court further orders that if there is no opposition by February 11, 2020, the demurrer will be sustained in its entirety.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
